Container.Name: K-Sorter
Container.Overview:
K-Sorter calmly sorts a messy folder of K-pop videos into correctly-named group (and member) folders, from an easy frosted-glass web UI. Sort once, sort TWICE.
Point it at a source folder and a destination folder, and it reads each filename, matches it to a group — and, for solo fancams/focus videos, to a member — then sorts it. Confident matches move automatically; only the ones it isn't sure about ask you a quick yes-or-yes. Nothing is ever renamed, nothing is overwritten, and every move can be undone.
Korean names, nicknames and former members are all handled (seeded from the open CC0 kpopnet dataset). Collabs go to a "_Special Stages" folder and are replicated into each group; sub-units get their own folder; suspected duplicates are flagged (never deleted); and a watch-folder can auto-sort new drops.
⚠️ SAME FILESYSTEM = INSTANT: keep your Source and Destination under the SAME share (e.g. both under /media). Moves on the same filesystem are atomic renames (instant, no copy). Moving across filesystems (or between different shares / network mounts) safely copies → verifies → deletes, which is slower but never loses or corrupts a file.
- Recursive scan, including nested subfolders - Confident auto-sort; precise prompts only when unsure - Undo any batch from the move history - Learns from your corrections so it gets smarter over time - Safe-mode streaming jobs keep huge libraries responsive
Container.Description: K-Sorter — a calm, accurate K-pop video sorter. Point it at a messy folder and a destination; it sorts each video into the right group (and member) folder, only asking when it isn't sure. Every move is undoable.

Config.Name: Source Folder
Config.Description: The messy folder to sort. The app scans this; inside the app just enter /source. TIP: keep this on the SAME share as the Destination so moves are instant atomic renames. Network shares work too — mount them on the Unraid host first, then browse to the host path here.

Config.Name: Destination Folder
Config.Description: Where the sorted group/member folders are created. Inside the app, enter /destination. Keep it on the same share as Source for instant moves; a different disk/share is still safe (copy → verify → delete) but slower.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the group database, logs, CSV exports and the undo journal.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for created files/folders. Unraid default is 99 (nobody).

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for created files/folders. Unraid default is 100 (users).

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: File-creation mask for new folders/files (022 = drwxr-xr-x / -rw-r--r--).

Config.Name: Watch Folder
Config.Description: Optional. Map a folder here to enable watch mode — new videos dropped in are auto-sorted (confident matches only; uncertain ones are left in place). Adding this shows a folder-browse dropdown. Leave unmapped to disable watch mode.

Config.Name: Watch Destination
Config.Description: Optional. Where watch-folder videos are sorted into. Required only if Watch Folder is mapped.

Config.Name: Auto-sort Threshold
Config.Description: Match score (0-100) at or above which a video is sorted automatically. Higher = more cautious (more goes to the confirm queue).

Config.Name: Confirm Threshold
Config.Description: Match score (0-100) at or above which a video is queued for your confirmation; below this it goes to the manual queue. Must be lower than the auto-sort threshold.

Config.Name: Verify Checksum
Config.Description: Extra paranoia for cross-filesystem moves: SHA-256 verify each file after copying (in addition to the always-on size check). Slower on large videos — leave 'false' unless you really want it.
